title: dryopteris intermedia var. concordiana
intro: Dryopteris intermedia var. concordiana, also known as Eastern Wood Fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris intermedia species, a type of fern that is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Eastern Wood Fern include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Eastern Wood Fern can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine as a poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
